question_text: An object has a mass of 2400 g and a volume of 3000 cm³. Predict whether this object will float or sink in water (density of water = 1 g/cm³), and justify your answer using a density calculation.
correct_answer: The object will float. Density = mass ÷ volume = 2400 g ÷ 3000 cm³ = 0.8 g/cm³. Since the object's density (0.8 g/cm³) is less than the density of water (1 g/cm³), the object will float.
explanation: To predict whether an object floats or sinks, you need to calculate its density and compare it to the density of the fluid. Using the formula density = mass ÷ volume, this object has a density of 0.8 g/cm³. Since this is less than water's density of 1 g/cm³, the object is less dense than water and will float. Objects float when they are less dense than the liquid they are in, and sink when they are more dense.
